author: manunlp
content:
Is there a plan to launch a "real" devnet (not a kurtosis one) for opt proofs?
If so, should we target fulu? (If I'm not wrong right now only LH and Prysm are ready and both targeting Fulu.)

Or should we wait to have a completely stabilized gloas devnet then we can run a opt proofs devnet targeting Gloas?
(cc <@402080271563751435> <@412614104222531604> )

author: manunlp
content: Maybe also dev / pandops resources are fully committed to Gloas right now so it's not the good time to run a opt proofs devnet at all, which is totally understandable.

author: barnabasbusa
content: i really don’t wanna deal with fulu anymore if anything it should be rebased on top of gloas and added to a glamsterdam devnet cycle if anyone wanna send some proofs imo

author: taulepton_
content: Let's do an assessment of the effort required to rebase on gloas starting with the consensus specs. One concern is that the client implementations for Gloas are not stable, so moving our implementations over to Gloas may be premature. What are your thoughts <@531934490826768418>

author: 0xrubo
content: <@774033563732541451> hey. for the stateless execution testing *only*, all the json fields in the zkevm fixtures can be ignored but the `statelessInputBytes` and `statelessOutputBytes`, correct?
